metadata.title: Set up Spring Boot Modulith Architecture

story.iWant: establish Spring Boot modulith architecture with explicit module boundaries and build-time verification tests
story.soThat: we can enforce architectural constraints automatically and prevent unintended coupling between domain modules

tasks.task: Configure build.gradle.kts with Spring Modulith dependencies
Create module package structure for 8 modules
Add ArchUnit tests for package access enforcement

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: All Java packages use io.monosense.synergyflow.* namespace convention
package-info.java created for each of the 8 modules with @ApplicationModule annotations declaring explicit allowedDependencies
ModularityTests.java passes, verifying module boundaries and detecting cyclic dependencies at build time
ArchUnitTests.java enforces that classes in internal/ packages are NOT accessible from outside their module, while api/ and spi/ packages are public

docs.doc: Complete Spring Modulith implementation guide with @ApplicationModule examples for all 8 modules (itsm, pm, workflow, security, eventing, sla, sse, audit), module dependency matrix showing allowed dependencies, @NamedInterface SPI pattern examples, and ModularityTests.java/ArchUnitTests.java verification test code samples

Complete backend directory structure showing all 8 module packages under io.monosense.synergyflow/, with subdirectories for each module: api/ (REST controllers), spi/ (Service Provider Interfaces with @NamedInterface), internal/ (package-private implementation), events/ (domain events)

Story 1.2 implementation tasks: Create SynergyFlowApplication.java, configure build.gradle.kts with Spring Boot 3.5.x and Spring Modulith dependencies, create 8 module packages with subdirectories, add package-info.java with @ApplicationModule annotations, create spi/ packages with @NamedInterface, add ModularityTests.java and ArchUnit tests to verify module boundaries

AC validation for Story 1.2: All packages start with io.monosense.synergyflow, package-info.java created for each module with @ApplicationModule, ModularityTests.java passes verifying module boundaries, ArchUnit test enforces internal/ package access rules

Backend technology stack: Spring Boot 3.4+, Java 21 LTS, Gradle 8.10+ with Kotlin DSL, Spring Modulith 1.2+, Spring Data JPA, Hibernate 7.0, JUnit 5 + Mockito for testing, ArchUnit for architecture tests

Architectural decision to adopt Spring Modulith core application with explicit module boundaries (@ApplicationModule) and three companion deployments (Event Worker, Timer/SLA, Indexer). Rationale: cohesive domain refactoring and fast iteration while isolating physics-heavy loops from HTTP request path. Requires strong module enforcement via build-time verification tests.

Testing standards carried forward from Story 1.1: Story 1.2 is the first story to add automated tests (JUnit 5 + Mockito + Spring Boot Test + ArchUnit). Future stories will follow unit test pattern: backend tests in backend/src/test/java/, frontend tests in frontend/src/__tests__/

file.reason: Existing Spring Boot application entry point from Story 1.1. Will scan for @ApplicationModule annotated packages when Spring context starts. No modification needed for Story 1.2.

Existing Gradle build configuration from Story 1.1 with Spring Boot 3.4.0, Spring Modulith BOM 1.2.4 already configured. Story 1.2 will add spring-modulith-starter-core, spring-modulith-starter-test, and archunit-junit5 dependencies.

code.note: This is Story 1.2 - creating module structure. No existing module code to reference. Story 1.2 CREATES the module packages and verification tests that all future stories will use.

tool.name: Gradle Wrapper
tool.note: gradlew, gradlew.bat, gradle/wrapper/ files present from Story 1.1

framework.name: Spring Boot
framework.note: Already configured in build.gradle.kts from Story 1.1

framework.name: Spring Modulith
framework.note: BOM already present; Story 1.2 adds starter-core and starter-test

framework.name: JUnit
framework.note: JUnit Platform Launcher already configured from Story 1.1

framework.name: ArchUnit
framework.note: Story 1.2 adds archunit-junit5 for architecture tests

constraints.constraint: Spring Modulith Module Boundaries: All 8 modules (itsm, pm, workflow, security, eventing, sla, sse, audit) MUST use @ApplicationModule annotation on package-info.java with explicit allowedDependencies declaration. Module dependency violations MUST cause build failure.

Module Dependency Matrix: itsm → {security, eventing, sla, sse, audit}; pm → {security, eventing, sse, audit}; workflow → {itsm.spi, pm.spi, security, eventing, audit}; sse → {eventing}; security, eventing, sla, audit → NO module dependencies (leaf modules). Workflow MUST depend on itsm.spi and pm.spi interfaces, NOT itsm/pm internal implementations.

Package Namespace: ALL Java code MUST use base package io.monosense.synergyflow for consistency. Module packages are direct children: io.monosense.synergyflow.itsm, io.monosense.synergyflow.pm, etc. No exceptions.

Module Package Structure: Each module MUST have subdirectories: api/ (public REST controllers), spi/ (Service Provider Interfaces with @NamedInterface for cross-module communication), internal/ (package-private implementation details), events/ (domain events published by module). This structure is MANDATORY for all modules.

Build-Time Verification: ModularityTests.java and ArchUnitTests.java MUST pass for ./gradlew build to succeed. These tests enforce architectural constraints automatically. Violations MUST fail the build, not just produce warnings. This is critical for preventing architectural drift.

Toolchain Versions: Spring Boot 3.4+, Spring Modulith 1.2+, Java 21 LTS (no earlier versions), Gradle 8.10+ with Kotlin DSL mandatory. @ApplicationModule and @NamedInterface annotations require Spring Modulith 1.2+ for full support.

Modulith Pattern: Explicit module boundaries via @ApplicationModule with strong build-time enforcement. Rationale: enables cohesive domain refactoring while preventing unintended coupling. Companions (Event Worker, Timer/SLA, Indexer) will be added in future stories but core modulith structure must be established first.

story-context.interfaces:
Spring Modulith annotation placed on package-info.java to declare module boundaries and allowed dependencies. Example: @ApplicationModule(displayName = "ITSM Module", allowedDependencies = { "security", "eventing", "sla", "sse", "audit" })
Spring Modulith annotation for SPI packages to expose specific interfaces for cross-module communication. Placed on spi/package-info.java. Example: @NamedInterface("spi") with JavaDoc explaining the interface contract.
<interface name="ApplicationModules" kind="test-api" signature="ApplicationModules.of(Class
